title: ranunculus apenninus
intro: Ranunculus apenninus, also known as Apennine buttercup, is a plant species in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) that grows in the Apennine Mountains of Italy. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and infections. The health benefits of Ranunculus apenninus include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used externally to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall health and well-being.
